ddls:
CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the individual */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Gender of the individual */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the education level */,
  "education_num" INTEGER /* Numeric representation of education level */,
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the marital status */,
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the work class */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the occupation */,
  "hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Number of hours worked per week */,
  "income_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the income level */,
  "label" INTEGER /* Binary label representing income level (0 for <=50K, 1 for >50K) */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of residence */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of residence */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of residence */,
  "employment_type" TEXT /* Type of employment */,
  "family_size" INTEGER /* Number of family members */,
  "family_status" TEXT /* Status of family (single, married, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_income_id FOREIGN KEY ("income_id") REFERENCES income_levels ("income_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_marital_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("marital_status_id") REFERENCES marital_statuses ("marital_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_workclass_id FOREIGN KEY ("workclass_id") REFERENCES workclasses ("workclass_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  "education_category" TEXT /* Category of the education level (e.g., high school, college, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_statuses" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status_name" TEXT /* Name of the marital status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marital status */,
  "marital_status_category" TEXT /* Category of the marital status (e.g., single, married, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workclasses" (
  "workclass_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work class */,
  "workclass_name" TEXT /* Name of the work class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the work class */,
  "workclass_category" TEXT /* Category of the work class (e.g., private, government, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workclass_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the occupation */,
  "occupation_category" TEXT /* Category of the occupation (e.g., service, professional, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "income_levels" (
  "income_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income level */,
  "income_name" TEXT /* Name of the income level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the income level */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Range of the income level (e.g., <=50K, >50K, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the demographic record */,
  "predicted_income_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the predicted income level */,
  "confidence" REAL /* Confidence of the prediction */,
  "model_used" TEXT /* Machine learning model used to make the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_predicted_income_id FOREIGN KEY ("predicted_income_id") REFERENCES income_levels ("income_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES demographics ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prediction_features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feature */,
  "feature_name" TEXT /* Name of the feature */,
  "feature_type" TEXT /* Type of the feature (e.g., categorical, numerical, etc.) */,
  "feature_description" TEXT /* Description of the feature */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prediction_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., supervised, unsupervised, etc.) */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prediction_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result */,
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the prediction */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the prediction (e.g., correct, incorrect, etc.) */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prediction_results_prediction_id FOREIGN KEY ("prediction_id") REFERENCES predictions ("prediction_id")
)
